input.headline: How to connect with old friends and why it matters

raw.headline: How to connect with old friends and why it matters

winner: I tried reconnecting with an old friend by digging up his Facebook profile — turns out he's been living under a tree and hasn't had internet since 2013. I guess we're both still friends, but now we're also both slightly confused.

candidates.text: I tried reconnecting with an old friend, so I dug up his childhood address—only to realize he'd literally left his number in the ground all along.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with a clever play on 'left his number in the ground,' concise and specific, with natural humor."}

candidates.text: I tried reconnecting with an old friend by digging up his Facebook profile — turns out he's been living under a tree and hasn't had internet since 2013. I guess we're both still friends, but now we're also both slightly confused.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, specific, and includes a surprising twist with the friend living under a tree without internet. It's relatable and uses natural language effectively."}

candidates.text: Why did my old friend get blocked? I guess he needed to *reconnect* his Wi-Fi!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke uses a pun on 'reconnect' but is somewhat generic and relies on a common tech reference, limiting its surprise and specificity."}

candidates.text: Why did my old friend block me on social media? I guess reconnecting with people isn’t just about sending a ‘hey, long time no see,’—it’s about not getting the cold shoulder when you do.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat relatable and has a mild twist, but it's a bit generic and lacks the sharp surprise or specificity that would make it stand out in a humor competition."}

candidates.text: To connect with my old friends, I bought a high-voltage industrial soldering iron and a bucket of copper wire, but I am still struggling to find a stable signal to my high school roommate.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke has a surprising twist with the soldering iron and copper wire, but the connection to the headline is weak and the humor feels forced."}

candidates.text: The best way to reconnect with old friends is to send them a LinkedIn request and then pretend you didn't know they were your friend in high school.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but the humor feels a bit generic and relies on a common stereotype about LinkedIn. It lacks originality and has a slightly passive-aggressive tone that may not land as well with all audiences."}

candidates.text: Reconnecting with old friends is important because it helps you maintain your "history," which is the only way to ensure your past doesn't become "past-tense.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Playful wordplay on 'history' and 'past-tense' adds surprise and specificity, with concise delivery and natural English."}

candidates.text: I finally reached out to my old college friends to reconnect and find common ground, but it turns out the only thing we have in common is that we both have no idea who they are anymore.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke has a decent setup and a somewhat surprising twist, but the punchline feels slightly forced and lacks specificity. The humor is mild and relies on a common experience, which makes it relatable but not particularly original or sharp."}

candidates.text: How do you reconnect with old friends? Just send them a message saying, "Hey, remember when we used to chat about stuff and now we're just remembering?
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with its wordplay on 'remember,' but it's a bit generic and lacks the punchy surprise of a stronger joke. It also feels slightly forced in its delivery."}
